question.en: Describe a time you were pressured to take a shortcut — by a timeline, a manager, or the rest of the team — and chose not to. What did 'not taking it' actually look like in practice?
question.es: Describe una ocasión en la que te presionaron para tomar un atajo — por un plazo, un gerente o el resto del equipo — y decidiste no tomarlo. ¿Cómo se veía en la práctica 'no tomarlo'?

must_include: the specific shortcut proposed — what exactly would have been bypassed or skipped, not just 'a corner was being cut'
the source and nature of the pressure — who was pushing, why, and what the consequence of not complying was stated to be

the principle or policy the candidate was holding — named explicitly, not just 'I felt it was wrong' (e.g., 'bypassing identity verification would violate our KYC obligations' or 'shipping without disclosure review exposed customers to misleading terms')
what 'not taking the shortcut' required the candidate to do differently — the concrete alternative path they proposed or executed, not just the refusal
the named cost of declining — to the timeline, the relationship with the manager or team, or the candidate's own standing — as a specific fact, not a generic acknowledgment of difficulty
must_avoid: framing the shortcut refusal as a technical quality call ('I didn't want to accrue tech debt') rather than an ethical or compliance obligation — that is an Excellence story, not a DTRT story
claiming the shortcut was never that tempting — DTRT stories require genuine temptation, because the bar only matters when there is pressure to lower it
blaming the manager or team for proposing the shortcut without naming the candidate's own role in the response — 'I pushed back' with no description of how is insufficient
